title: Cobra Is Forced Below
description: On pages 12–16, this part focuses on cobra is forced below, keeping the scene centered on the conflict, choices, and stakes of the Alabasta arc.
scene: Cobra Is Forced Below. Crocodile drags the conflict toward the royal tomb and the truth of Pluton while Robin watches the plan unfold. This section presents the immediate scene beat before the dialogue continues.

dialogue.speaker: Crocodile
dialogue.text: I have to say that the

dialogue.speaker: Miss All Sunday
dialogue.text: Vivi...

dialogue.speaker: Miss All Sunday
dialogue.text: 15 minutes left until the explosion...

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: More rebel rein- forecasts are coming to the palace square as we speak...

dialogue.speaker: King Cobra
dialogue.text: All the while oblivious to the fact that they're walking to their very graves...

dialogue.speaker: King Cobra
dialogue.text: Furthermore, I expected

dialogue.speaker: Vivi
dialogue.text: If you had just told the Royalists earlier that the palace square would be blown up soon,

dialogue.speaker: Zoro
dialogue.text: Even if it did cause a panic, I'm sure it would've saved a few thousand to a ten thousand lives at least...

dialogue.speaker: Zoro
dialogue.text: No, stop! Crocodile, stop!

dialogue.speaker: Zoro
dialogue.text: Your deluded idealism that wished to save everyone,

dialogue.speaker: Pell
dialogue.text: is what inevitably led to the deaths of your beloved people.

dialogue.speaker: Crocodile
dialogue.text: This is... Dance Powder!

dialogue.speaker: Chaka
dialogue.text: From the very beginning to the very end, the people of this country have provided me no shortage of laughs...!

dialogue.speaker: King Cobra
dialogue.text: No! Absolutely not! This is not a game!

dialogue.speaker: Crocodile
dialogue.text: You worked very hard these past 2 years,

dialogue.speaker: Crocodile
dialogue.text: Spying on my organi- zation,

dialogue.speaker: Miss All Sunday
dialogue.text: It's me! Can't you tell? Well, I did lose a bit of weight so I guess I can't blame you.

dialogue.speaker: Zoro
dialogue.text: But in the end, you and your comrades couldn't stop anything at all.

dialogue.speaker: King Cobra
dialogue.text: All your idealistic talk of stopping the rebellion or saving this country,

dialogue.speaker: Alabasta rebel
dialogue.text: Only ever amounted to increasing the casualties...!

dialogue.speaker: Alabasta rebel
dialogue.text: Let me make this clear to you.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: You cannot save this country.

dialogue.speaker: Crocodile
dialogue.text: I am

dialogue.speaker: Alabasta rebel
dialogue.text: It can't be...!

summary: This part develops cobra is forced below and pushes the Alabasta conflict closer to its next decisive turn.
